label: point-boundary
headline_statement: Refuted: the three-rank finite-support panel in \(\S13\) satisfies Assumptions~\ref{ass:consistency}--\ref{ass:exactfit}, \ref{ass:convexfit}, \ref{ass:rank}, and \ref{ass:affineinterior}, has \(z(u)\notin\operatorname{rowspan}\bar X(u)\) on \((1/3,2/3]\), but \(\mathcal{Q}_{DW}(P)=\{0\}\) and \(\mathcal{F}_{DW}(P)\) is singleton.
witness_construction_if_refuted: Use J=3, T0=1, x0(u)=0, and three rank blocks I_L=(0,1/3], I_M=(1/3,2/3], I_H=(2/3,1). Set X=(-2,-1,0), z=(-2,-1,0) on I_L; X=(-1,0,1), z=(0,0,1) on I_M; and X=(0,1,2), z=(0,1,2) on I_H. The middle block has affine null direction h=(1,-2,1) with z^T h=1 and convex relative interior, but affine feasible values are fixed at 0 on I_L and I_H. Monotonicity then forces every donor-weight compatible quantile to equal 0 on I_M as well, so Q_DW(P)={0} and F_DW(P) is singleton despite positive-measure row-span failure.
